name: VS Code Internal Command MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server that exposes VS Code's internal command palette and APIs to AI agents, enabling agents to programmatically execute VS Code commands — opening files, running build tasks, executing debugger actions, managing extensions, and controlling editor state via MCP tool calls.
use_cases: Automating VS Code editor actions from AI coding agents
Triggering build and test tasks from CI/CD automation agents
Opening and navigating files programmatically from code review agents
Controlling VS Code debugger from automated testing agents
not_for: Environments without VS Code (JetBrains, Vim, Emacs users need different tools)
Production server automation (VS Code is a desktop IDE, not a server tool)
Teams needing a language server protocol (LSP) integration (different abstraction layer)
best_when: An AI coding agent running inside VS Code needs to trigger editor actions directly — closing the loop between agent suggestions and IDE execution.
avoid_when: You're not using VS Code, or your agent workflow doesn't need direct editor control — most code generation tasks don't require IDE integration.

auth.notes: No authentication — runs within VS Code process context. Access controlled by VS Code itself.

pricing.notes: Free open source VS Code extension/MCP server.

performance.rate_limits: Local — no rate limits; bounded by VS Code responsiveness

known_agent_gotchas: Requires VS Code to be running — server is unavailable when editor is closed
Internal VS Code APIs can change between VS Code versions — check version compatibility
Some commands may trigger UI dialogs that block agent execution
Limited to VS Code command palette commands — cannot access raw editor internals
agent_readiness.security_notes: Runs within VS Code process. Can execute any VS Code command including file operations and extension management. Limit to trusted agent connections only. Local — no external data transmission.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local VS Code process access. All VS Code commands accessible — including file deletion and extension management. Trust only controlled agents.
